message:
harden(runtime_compat): centralize Unix signal delivery through a validated helper

Replace every ad-hoc `std::process::Command::new("kill")` call site with two
new helpers in `runtime_compat::process`:

  * `send_unix_signal_to_pid(pid, signal)` — targets a single PID
  * `send_unix_signal_to_process_group(pgid, signal)` — targets `-pgid`

Both helpers:

- Resolve `kill` via an absolute path (`/bin/kill` or `/usr/bin/kill`) and
  refuse to fall back to anything `$PATH` provides, eliminating a PATH
  injection vector for anything running us under a hostile environment.
- Validate the target (positive PID required; zero or negative rejected
  with `InvalidInput`), so a stray `-1` or `0` cannot accidentally
  broadcast a signal to every process the UID owns.
- Validate the signal name: only non-empty ASCII alphanumerics (e.g.
  `TERM`, `KILL`, `HUP`, `0`) are accepted. Anything containing shell
  metacharacters, spaces, option-like strings (`-9`, `--help`), or
  numeric-with-punctuation payloads like `"TERM;sh"` is rejected before
  `kill` ever sees it.
- Return `std::io::Result<ExitStatus>` so callers keep the same error
  ergonomics they had with `Command::status()`.

Migrated call sites:

- `frankenterm/src/main.rs`:
    * `stop_mux_server_processes` (SIGTERM fan-out to initial PIDs)
    * `run()` watcher shutdown path (SIGTERM then optional SIGKILL)
    * `handle_rules_profile_command` (SIGHUP to running watcher)
- `frankenterm-core/src/network_observer.rs::terminate_rano_child`
- `frankenterm-core/src/orphan_reaper.rs::scan_and_reap` (both the
  default and cx-aware variants; also drops the intermediate
  `pid_str` clone since the helper owns formatting)
- `frankenterm-core/src/session_resume.rs::kill_casr_process_tree`
- `frankenterm-core/src/subprocess_bridge.rs` process-group killer

Net effect: one audited boundary for spawning `kill`, consistent input
validation, and no behavioural change for valid inputs — SIGKILL to
`-child.id()` and SIGTERM to a PID still do exactly what they did before.

Also adds two unit tests:

- `unix_signal_helper_uses_fixed_kill_path_and_rejects_injected_signal`
  asserts the resolved path is absolute and starts with `/`, and that
  a shell-metacharacter-bearing signal name (`"TERM;sh"`) is rejected
  with `InvalidInput` instead of reaching `kill`.
- `unix_signal_helper_can_probe_current_process` uses signal `0` to
  verify the happy path against our own PID without actually killing
  anything.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
